title: The One Where They Get Back Together

content.problemStatement:
**F.R.I.E.N.D.S** stars Jennifer Aniston, Courteney Cox, Lisa Kudrow, Matt LeBlanc, Matthew Perry and David Schwimmer have come together for the much-awaited reunion. The whole cast is going to shoot for the episode on the sets of their show. Being a huge fan, Chef's family is excited to watch the live shoot. 



The set has infinite rows with infinite seats in each row for the audience. The seats are numbered starting from 111 as shown in the image above. Let the seat number of the seat in the ithi^{th}ith row and jthj^{th}jth column be Si,jS_{i,j}Si,jÔÇő. The price of a seat is equal to the seat number of that particular seat.

Chef wants to reserve exactly NNN seats for his family members such that the reserved seats form a rectangle and all the family members stay together.

Chef is allowed to choose a reduction price XXX. After choosing the value of reduction price; if Chef has chosen the seat in the ithi^{th}ith row and jthj^{th}jth column, the price of this seat becomes ÔłúSi,jÔłĺXÔłú|S_{i,j} - X|ÔłúSi,jÔÇőÔłĺXÔłú for Chef. The total price of the seats selected by Chef is equal to the sum of individual prices of seats (after applying the reduction price).

Find the **minimum possible total price** Chef will have to pay for a selection of seats which form a rectangle of area exactly NNN. Since this number can be huge, print it modulo (109+710^9+7109+7).


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains of a single line of input, one integer NNN, denoting the number of people in Chef's family.


Output Format
For each testcase, output in a single line the minimum price Chef has to pay for reserving NNN seats modulo (109+710^9+7109+7).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (1 ÔëĄ N ÔëĄ 400000) ÔÇô the exact number of seats Chef wants to reserve.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum possible total price Chef has to pay modulo 1000000007.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: One of the possible optimal selections would be seat number 555 and XXX = 555. Cost equals |seat number - X|, i.e.  000.
Test case 2: Chef needs 444 seats. Some possible selections are shown through rectangles in red, purple and green.


Seats coloured in purple: [2,4,7,11][2, 4, 7, 11][2,4,7,11]. If Chef chooses XXX = 222, Cost is |222 - 222| + |444 - 222| + |777 - 222| + |111111 - 222| = 161616. For XXX = 111111, Cost is |222 - 111111| + |444 - 111111| + |777 - 111111| + |111111 - 111111| = 202020.
Seats coloured in red: [1,2,3,5][1, 2, 3, 5][1,2,3,5]. If Chef chooses XXX = 111, Cost is |111 - 111| + |222 - 111| + |333 - 111| + |555 - 111| = 777. For XXX = 333, Cost is |111 - 333| + |222 - 333| + |333 - 333| + |555 - 333| = 555. We can calculate the cost for other values of XXX similarly.

In this case, one of the optimal selections would be seats coloured in red, with XXX = 333.

solution.approach:
For a rectangle of a rows and b columns (a┬Ěb = N) the seat number at position (i, j) (1ÔÇĹbased) is given by the Cantor pairing formula: S(i, j) = ((i + j - 2) * (i + j - 1)) / 2 + i.  For a fixed shape (a, b) we place the rectangle with its topÔÇĹleft corner at (1, 1) ÔÇô this gives the smallest possible spread of numbers, and any translation only adds the same constant to every seat, which does not change the sum of absolute deviations from the median.  For each divisor a of N we set b = N / a, generate all a┬Ěb seat numbers using the formula, sort them, pick the median (any median works for even count) and compute the sum of absolute differences to that median.  The answer for N is the minimum sum over all divisor pairs.  Because N ÔëĄ 4┬Ě10ÔüÁ the total amount of numbers generated for a single test case is at most N, which easily fits into memory.  The algorithm runs in O(N log N) per divisor pair, but the number of divisor pairs is at most about 2┬ĚÔłÜN (Ôëł 1300 for the maximal N).  In practice this is fast enough for the given limits.

Steps per test case:
1. Enumerate all divisors a of N (a ÔëĄ ÔłÜN). For each a, let b = N / a.
2. For the shape (a, b) generate the list `vals` of size N using the formula S(i, j).
3. Sort `vals` and take `median = vals[N//2]` (0ÔÇĹbased indexing).
4. Compute `cost = ╬ú |v - median|` (use 64ÔÇĹbit integers).
5. Keep the minimum `cost` over all shapes.
6. Output `cost % MOD` where MOD = 1_000_000_007.

The same procedure is repeated for the transposed shape (b, a) ÔÇô it is automatically covered when the divisor loop reaches the larger divisor.

Complexities:
- Time: O( ╬ú_{d|N} N log N ) in the worst case, but with N ÔëĄ 4┬Ě10ÔüÁ and at most ~1300 divisor pairs this easily fits within the time limit.
- Space: O(N) for the temporary list of seat numbers.

The implementation is provided in five languages, each following the same logic.
solution.timeComplexity: O(D * N log N) per test case, where D is the number of divisor pairs of N (D ÔëĄ 2┬ĚÔłÜN).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) auxiliary space for the list of seat numbers.
